title: Level 6.66
wikitext:
{{Blurbackground|file=Level_6.66.jpg}} {{CSS|content= /* Page */ .page { border: solid 2px #ffffff; margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border-radius: 5px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 8px 0 #ffffff; } /* Darkmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: #000000;; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #1a1a1a; --theme-link-color: #ffffff; --theme-border-color: #ffffff; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px #ffffff; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px #ffffff; } --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: #ffffff; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: #ffffff; } /* Lightmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: #000000; --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #1a1a1a; --theme-link-color: #ffffff; --theme-border-color: #ffffff; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px #ffffff; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px #ffffff; } --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #000000; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: #ffffff; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: #ffffff; }< /nowiki>}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 6.66: "Cryophobia" }}:
{{Class Custom Image|bg-image=Level_6.66.jpg |class=5e - Environmental |safety=Unsecure| entity=Extremely Low Temperatures| hexagon=E| security=Unsafe|text-color=white}}
'''''Level 6.66''' is a sublevel between [[Level 6]] and [[Level 7]].''
''Description''
{{Blurimage|file=Level_6.66.jpg|caption=The first photo taken of '''Level 6.66.'''}} '''Level 6.66''', also known as "'''Cryophobia'''" resembles a large abandoned basement. The level consists of small rooms and extremely long and narrow corridors. The entire place is dark, and the only source of luminosity is small lamps found in the ceiling of rooms and corridors. The current state of the level is decaying, and the floor, walls, and ceiling look burnt and destroyed. Nothing can be found in the rooms except the old lamps shining weak white light that fades into the deep darkness of '''Level 6.66'''. The atmosphere of the level is surprisingly cold, having extremely low temperatures, causing any individual who stays at '''Level 6.66''' without proper equipment to die from hypothermia. To avoid problems with the frigid atmosphere of '''Level 6.66''' it is highly recommended that individuals keep close to the lights while exploring the rooms because light is the only heat source in the vast space of the level. Due to its temperature, '''Level 6.66''' is devoid of life. '''Level 6.66''' is unsafe and has unstable properties, all of which are related to the temperature in the atmosphere, which is usually cold. The entire level always seems to be changing due to its temperature; these climate changes can indicate a decrease in the temperature or a low increase. Due to these exchanges, the level may become unstable for long periods until it adapts to the changes. Certain events may indicate that abrupt weather changes will occur. Walls starting to move or appear loose, becoming narrower or wider; Small leaks of boiling water from the ceiling or floor; Rooms constantly repeating; Lights flickering. When these events occur, individuals should prepare for a sudden climate change and try to seek shelter. After spending a considerable amount of time on '''Level 6.66''', the coldness will begin to take hold of the wanderer's mind, and consequently, hallucinations will occur. Hallucinations can be accompanied by symptoms such as nausea, voices, and whispers. If voices start becoming frequent, the individual will need to leave the level as soon as possible. Traveling far enough into '''Level 6.66''' may cause wanderers to find stairs that lead down. However, it is impossible to explore these areas because, for inexplicable reasons, they are completely flooded by boiling water. Even if these waters seem clean, consumption is not recommended. '''Level 6.66''' is extremely hard to explore and stay in it. As a consequence, its climate-changing properties, along with its massive structure and what lies after it, have never been explained.
''The Blackout''
Between large and unknown periods in '''level 6.66''', the wanderer may hear strange noises coming from the lights, and after these noises, all lights on the level will go out, making the entire level completely dark. This is a fatal event due to the high decrease in the current temperature of the level. At that moment, the entire observable space of '''Level 6.66''' will become a vast abyss, and consequently, the temperature will begin to decrease, causing the wanderer to begin to hallucinate and hear voices. It is reported that these voices are different from voices heard when the lights are on. The voices will begin to call the wanderer to visit the underground area of the level, which is flooded. When the lights turn off, it is recommended that the wanderer not listen to the voices and sit in a corner and try to warm up until the lights come back again. The lights usually come back 3 hours after the blackout.
''The Drought Underground''
{{Blurimage|file=Underground area of level 6.66.webp|caption=Photo of the Underground area inside '''Level 6.66.'''}} After long periods in '''Level 6.66''', loud sounds of water flowing out may be heard, during these sounds, water from the underground areas will begin to drain, and these areas will become suitable for exploration, however, it's not recommended to stay in the underground for a long time, because, after a random time, the water will rapidly rise again. The underground of '''Level 6.66''' is different from the original area and consists of long and extremely narrow passages that intersect perpendicularly into 4 paths at each junction, like a ventilation maze. These paths tend to remain constant ahead, but they may have slight and abrupt inclines upwards and downwards. inexplicably, these areas are less cold than the main floor; however, it is more advisable to stay on the upper floor. These areas do not have any light source, so a flashlight is required. In the underground, even with more suitable temperatures, it still seems devoid of life. However, the voices heard during the blackout will be more frequent here, and in an unknown manner, they seem to recognize anyone who enters the underground, and once again, invite them to explore the long tunnels even further. Many wanderers have gone missing by following the voices because they would be hearing family members or friends they had in the frontrooms. The interest of these voices and what lies beyond the underground is currently not known. However, it is recommended that any wanderer should stay away from the underground and should not listen to the voices. The underground should only be entered if the wanderer needs to warm up urgently, if necessary, one should not go away from the exit, as the water will rise again quickly, leading anyone who gets lost inside the tunnels to a worse fate.
''Entities''
'''Level 6.66''' is completely devoid of life; therefore, no entity has ever been seen on this level. The voices heard in the deepest zones are recorded only as hallucinations.
''Colonies and Outposts''
Due to the nature of this level, colonies and outposts are unable to be established.
''Entrances and Exits''
''Entrances''
•Exploring deep into colder areas of [[Level 6]] have a rare chance to take one here.
''Exits''
•Crawling through tunnels that lead upwards can take one to [[Level 7]]. •Crawling through tunnels that lead downwards can take one to [[Level 37.1]]. •Exploring darker areas can take one back to [[Level 6]].
